title: lactuca quercina subsp. quercina
intro: Lactuca quercina subsp. quercina, also known as wild lettuce or prickly lettuce, is a biennial plant in the dandelion tribe of the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Lactuca quercina subsp. quercina include its ability to act as a natural sedative and relaxant, due to its high content of lactucarium, a complex mixture of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds. The health benefits of consuming Lactuca quercina subsp. quercina include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting sleep, and alleviating symptoms of insomnia, while also exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Lactuca quercina subsp. quercina can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
